name: working-on-ancplua-plugins
description: Primary instruction manual for working within the ancplua-claude-plugins monorepo. Use when creating, modifying, or debugging plugins in this repository.
capabilities: Provides mandatory architectural rules and naming conventions for plugin development
Lists validation commands to verify plugin structure and syntax
Documents the correct directory layout for plugins, skills, and commands
References official Claude Code documentation for plugins, skills, and hooks
Describes Git conventions and commit message formats
Guides debugging steps for common plugin issues
author: ANcpLua
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill. The static analyzer flagged markdown code formatting (backticks) and documentation links as security issues. All 162 findings are false positives. This skill provides conventions, validation commands, and debugging steps in markdown only. No executable code, file system access, network calls, or environment variable access occurs.
valueStatement: This repository lacks consistent conventions for plugin development. Developers struggle to follow correct patterns for creating, validating, and publishing Claude Code plugins. This skill provides the single source of truth for all repository-specific workflows and architectural rules.
limitations: Only applies to ancplua-claude-plugins repository, not general plugin development
Does not execute validation scripts or modify files automatically
Does not provide code templates or generate plugin scaffolding

useCases.title: Create new plugins
useCases.description: Follow conventions to scaffold plugins with correct structure and manifest files

useCases.title: Validate changes
useCases.description: Run validation commands before submitting pull requests to the repository

useCases.title: Debug plugin issues
useCases.description: Diagnose why plugins fail to load or behave unexpectedly in Claude Code

promptTemplates.title: Quick validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Run the full validation suite for this repository using the commands in this skill

promptTemplates.title: Create new plugin
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new plugin structure with correct directory layout and manifest files following the conventions

promptTemplates.title: Debug plugin
promptTemplates.prompt: Debug why my plugin is not loading correctly in Claude Code using the testing procedures

promptTemplates.title: Follow conventions
promptTemplates.prompt: Ensure my changes follow the repository conventions and naming standards documented here

bestPractices: Always run local-validate.sh before pushing changes to catch issues early
Use ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} for paths in configuration files instead of hardcoded absolute paths
Follow conventional commit messages format for clear changelog generation and automation
antiPatterns: Skipping validation before committing changes allows broken plugins into the repository
Using absolute paths in plugin configurations breaks portability across different systems
Modifying .claude-plugin/ directory for skills, commands, or hooks violates the manifest-only purpose

faq.question: What tools does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill supports Claude Code. It is designed specifically for Claude Code plugin development workflows.

faq.question: What does the validation check?
faq.answer: Validation checks plugin manifests, shell scripts, markdown formatting, CI workflow syntax, and JSON validity. It does not test runtime behavior.

faq.question: How do I integrate with other plugins?
faq.answer: Reference the marketplace.json for available plugins. MCP tools and hooks can reference other plugins using ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} for portability.

faq.question: Is my data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This is a read-only documentation skill that provides guidelines. It does not access, modify, or transmit any user data.

faq.question: Why is my plugin not loading?
faq.answer: Common causes include invalid JSON, missing required fields, scripts not executable, or hardcoded paths. Run local-validate.sh and check Claude Code logs.

faq.question: How does this compare to official docs?
faq.answer: This skill supplements official Claude Code documentation with repository-specific conventions. Refer to code.claude.com for core functionality documentation.
